- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	"""Tool registry for ReAct analysis agent.
2	
3	Provides a registry of analysis tools that the LLM can browse and invoke.
4	Each tool wrapper tracks source_evidence_ids for citation provenance —
5	ensuring analysis results cite ORIGINAL sources, never "POLARIS Analysis Toolkit".
6	"""
7	
8	import asyncio
9	import logging
10	import os
11	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
12	from typing import Any, Callable, Optional
13	
14	logger = logging.getLogger("polaris_graph")
15	
16	
17	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
18	# Core data structures
19	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
20	
21	@dataclass
22	class ToolResult:
23	    """Result from executing an analysis tool.
24	
25	    The critical field is source_evidence_ids: it traces every statistic,
26	    chart, and insight back to the original evidence pieces that produced it.
27	    Synthesis uses these IDs to generate [CITE:ev_xxx] tokens.
28	    """
29	
30	    success: bool
31	    tool_name: str
32	    markdown: str = ""
33	    statistics: dict = field(default_factory=dict)
34	    charts: list[dict] = field(default_factory=list)
35	    source_evidence_ids: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
36	    data_points_produced: list[dict] = field(default_factory=list)
37	    insights: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
38	    error: str | None = None
39	
40	
41	@dataclass
42	class ToolDefinition:
43	    """Definition of an analysis tool available to the ReAct agent."""
44	
45	    name: str
46	    description: str
47	    requires_data: bool
48	    requires_llm: bool
49	    parameters: dict = field(default_factory=dict)
50	    execute: Optional[Callable] = None
51	    # Decide-prompt scaling (redesign "Scaling the decide step past 20 tools"): ``core`` tools are
52	    # ALWAYS listed in full; non-core tools collapse to a one-line-per-tool category INDEX once the
53	    # registry grows past ``core_threshold``. ``tags`` groups the index by category. Defaults keep
54	    # every existing call site byte-identical (no core marking, no tags => the pre-scaling menu).
55	    tags: list = field(default_factory=list)
56	    core: bool = False
57	
58	
59	class ToolRegistry:
60	    """Registry of analysis tools the LLM can pick from."""
61	
62	    def __init__(self):
63	        self._tools: dict[str, ToolDefinition] = {}
64	
65	    def register(self, tool: ToolDefinition) -> None:
66	        self._tools[tool.name] = tool
67	
68	    def get_tool(self, name: str) -> ToolDefinition | None:
69	        return self._tools.get(name)
70	
71	    def available_tools(self, has_data: bool) -> list[str]:
72	        """Return names of tools available given current data state."""
73	        return [
74	            name for name, tool in self._tools.items()
75	            if not tool.requires_data or has_data
76	        ]
77	
78	    def _describe_full(self, tool: "ToolDefinition", has_data: bool) -> list[str]:
79	        available = not tool.requires_data or has_data
80	        status = "" if available else " [UNAVAILABLE: needs numeric data first]"
81	        lines = [f"- {tool.name}: {tool.description}{status}"]
82	        if tool.parameters:
83	            for param, desc in tool.parameters.items():
84	                lines.append(f"    {param}: {desc}")
85	        return lines
86	
87	    def get_tool_descriptions(self, has_data: bool) -> str:
88	        """Format tool descriptions for LLM prompt (full listing — every tool + its params)."""
89	        lines: list[str] = []
90	        for name in sorted(self._tools.keys()):
91	            lines.extend(self._describe_full(self._tools[name], has_data))
92	        return "\n".join(lines)
93	
94	    def get_decide_menu(self, has_data: bool, core_threshold: int = 20) -> str:
95	        """The scaled decide-prompt menu (redesign 'Scaling the decide step past 20 tools').
96	
97	        At or below ``core_threshold`` tools this is BYTE-IDENTICAL to ``get_tool_descriptions``
98	        (today's ~13-tool registry prints in full, unchanged). Past the threshold it prints the
99	        CORE tools in full + a one-line-per-tool INDEX of the rest grouped by ``tags`` category, so
100	        the prompt stays compact as the toolkit grows to dozens/hundreds. Call ``list_tools`` to pull
101	        a non-core tool's full description + params on demand. Never elides a CORE tool; never hides
102	        a tool entirely (every non-core tool still appears in the index)."""
103	        if len(self._tools) <= core_threshold:
104	            return self.get_tool_descriptions(has_data)
105	        core_lines: list[str] = []
106	        index: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
107	        for name in sorted(self._tools.keys()):
108	            tool = self._tools[name]
109	            if tool.core:
110	                core_lines.extend(self._describe_full(tool, has_data))
111	                continue
112	            cat = (tool.tags[0] if tool.tags else "other")
113	            avail = "" if (not tool.requires_data or has_data) else " [needs numeric data]"
114	            gist = tool.description.split(". ")[0].split(" — ")[0][:90]
115	            index.setdefault(str(cat), []).append(f"    - {name}: {gist}{avail}")
116	        out = ["CORE tools (use directly):", *core_lines,
117	               "", "MORE tools (call `list_tools` with a name/category for full params):"]
118	        for cat in sorted(index):
119	            out.append(f"  [{cat}]")
120	            out.extend(sorted(index[cat]))
121	        return "\n".join(out)
122	
123	
124	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
125	# Provenance helpers
126	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
127	
128	def _extract_evidence_ids(data_points: list[dict]) -> list[str]:
129	    """Extract unique evidence IDs from a list of data points."""
130	    ids = []
131	    seen = set()
132	    for dp in data_points:
133	        ev_id = dp.get("evidence_id", "")
134	        if ev_id and ev_id not in seen:
135	            ids.append(ev_id)
136	            seen.add(ev_id)
137	    return ids
138	
139	
140	def _cite_inline(text: str, evidence_ids: list[str]) -> str:
141	    """Embed [CITE:ev_xxx] tokens on data-containing paragraphs.
142	
143	    Strategy: prefer paragraphs that contain numbers or data (those are
144	    the actual claims that need citations). Fall back to any content
145	    paragraph. Skip headers, table rows, and blank lines.
146	    """
147	    if not evidence_ids:
148	        return text
149	
150	    import re as _re
151	
152	    cite_ids = evidence_ids[:15]
153	
154	    paragraphs = text.split("\n\n")
155	
156	    # Score paragraphs: prefer those with numbers (actual data claims)
157	    scored = []
158	    for i, para in enumerate(paragraphs):
159	        stripped = para.strip()
160	        if not stripped:
161	            continue
162	        if stripped.startswith("|") or stripped.startswith("---"):
163	            continue  # Skip tables and separators
164	        if stripped.startswith("#"):
165	            continue  # Skip headers
166	
167	        # Data paragraphs have numbers with units
168	        has_data = bool(_re.search(
169	            r'\d+\.?\d*\s*(%|mg|ng|ppt|ppb|ppm|USD|\$|m2|kWh|g/L|mg/g)',
170	            stripped,
171	        ))
172	        # List items with data are good citation targets
173	        has_list_data = stripped.startswith("-") and bool(
174	            _re.search(r'\d+', stripped)
175	        )
176	        score = 2 if has_data else (1 if has_list_data else 0)
177	        scored.append((i, score))
178	
179	    # Sort by score descending, then by position (earlier = better)
180	    scored.sort(key=lambda x: (-x[1], x[0]))
181	
182	    # Distribute citations across top-scored paragraphs
183	    idx = 0
184	    assigned = set()
185	    for para_idx, _score in scored:
186	        if idx >= len(cite_ids):
187	            break
188	        if para_idx in assigned:
189	            continue
190	        batch = cite_ids[idx:idx + 3]
191	        tokens = "".join(f"[CITE:{eid}]" for eid in batch)
192	        paragraphs[para_idx] = paragraphs[para_idx].rstrip() + " " + tokens
193	        idx += len(batch)
194	        assigned.add(para_idx)
195	
196	    # If no data paragraphs found, attach to first content paragraph
197	    if idx == 0 and cite_ids:
198	        for i, para in enumerate(paragraphs):
199	            s = para.strip()
200	            if s and not s.startswith("|") and not s.startswith("#"):
201	                tokens = "".join(f"[CITE:{eid}]" for eid in cite_ids[:5])
202	                paragraphs[i] = para.rstrip() + " " + tokens
203	                break
204	
205	    return "\n\n".join(paragraphs)
206	
207	
208	def _evidence_ids_from_store(evidence_store: dict, limit: int = 10) -> list[str]:
209	    """Get evidence IDs from the store, excluding analysis entries."""
210	    return [
211	        eid for eid in list(evidence_store.keys())[:limit * 2]
212	        if evidence_store[eid].get("type") != "analysis"
213	    ][:limit]
214	
215	
216	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
217	# Tool wrapper functions (each tracks source_evidence_ids)
218	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
219	
220	async def _wrap_extract_numeric_data(
221	    evidence_store: dict,
222	    data_points: list[dict],
223	    client: Any,
224	    **kwargs,
225	) -> ToolResult:
226	    """Wrapper for evidence_extractor.extract_numbers_from_evidence().
227	
228	    Builds per-item citations: each data point gets a [CITE:ev_xxx] token
229	    from its source evidence, placed on the list item — not dumped on the
230	    summary header. This fixes the citation mismatch audit failure.
231	    """
232	    from src.polaris_graph.tools.evidence_extractor import (
233	        extract_numbers_from_evidence,
234	    )
235	
236	    extracted = extract_numbers_from_evidence(evidence_store)
237	    if not extracted:
238	        return ToolResult(
239	            success=False,
240	            tool_name="extract_numeric_data",
241	            markdown="No numeric data found in evidence.",
242	            error="No numeric values could be extracted",
243	        )
244	
245	    ev_ids = _extract_evidence_ids(extracted)
246	
247	    # Build per-item cited markdown (not generic summary)
248	    by_type: dict[str, list] = {}
249	    for dp in extracted:
250	        dt = dp.get("data_type", "unknown")
251	        by_type.setdefault(dt, []).append(dp)
252	
253	    lines = [f"**Extracted Data:** {len(extracted)} data points "
254	             f"from {len(ev_ids)} sources\n"]
255	    for dtype, items in sorted(by_type.items()):
256	        lines.append(f"- **{dtype}** ({len(items)} values):")
257	        for item in items[:5]:
258	            eid = item.get("evidence_id", "")
259	            cite = f" [CITE:{eid}]" if eid else ""
260	            val = item.get("value", "")
261	            unit = item.get("unit", "")
262	            label = item.get("label", "")[:50]
263	            lines.append(f"  - {label}: {val} {unit}{cite}")
264	        if len(items) > 5:
265	            lines.append(f"  - ... and {len(items) - 5} more")
266	
267	    units = sorted(set(dp.get("unit", "") for dp in extracted if dp.get("unit")))
268	    if units:
269	        lines.append(f"\n**Units:** {', '.join(units)}")
270	
271	    markdown = "\n".join(lines)
272	
273	    return ToolResult(
274	        success=True,
275	        tool_name="extract_numeric_data",
276	        markdown=markdown,
277	        source_evidence_ids=ev_ids,
278	        data_points_produced=extracted,
279	        insights=[
280	            f"Extracted {len(extracted)} numeric data points "
281	            f"from {len(ev_ids)} evidence pieces",
282	        ],
283	    )
284	
285	
286	async def _wrap_query_evidence_sql(
287	    evidence_store: dict,
288	    data_points: list[dict],
289	    client: Any,
290	    **kwargs,
291	) -> ToolResult:
292	    """Wrapper for EvidenceDatabase.query()."""
293	    from src.polaris_graph.tools.evidence_database import EvidenceDatabase
294	
295	    query_sql = kwargs.get("sql", "")
296	    if not query_sql:
297	        query_sql = (
298	            "SELECT quality_tier, COUNT(*) as n, "
299	            "ROUND(AVG(relevance_score), 3) as avg_rel "
300	            "FROM evidence GROUP BY quality_tier ORDER BY n DESC"
301	        )
302	
303	    db = EvidenceDatabase()
304	    loaded = db.load_evidence(evidence_store)
305	    if loaded == 0:
306	        db.close()
307	        return ToolResult(
308	            success=False,
309	            tool_name="query_evidence_sql",
310	            markdown="No evidence to query.",
311	            error="Evidence store empty",
312	        )
313	
314	    result = db.query(query_sql)
315	    db.close()
316	
317	    if not result["success"]:
318	        return ToolResult(
319	            success=False,
320	            tool_name="query_evidence_sql",
321	            markdown=f"SQL query failed: {result.get('error', 'unknown')}",
322	            error=result.get("error"),
323	        )
324	
325	    # Extract evidence_ids from result rows if the query returns them
326	    ev_ids = []
327	    if "evidence_id" in result.get("columns", []):
328	        idx = result["columns"].index("evidence_id")
329	        ev_ids = [str(row[idx]) for row in result.get("rows", []) if row[idx]]
330	
331	    if not ev_ids:
332	        ev_ids = _evidence_ids_from_store(evidence_store)
333	
334	    markdown = f"**SQL Query:** `{query_sql}`\n\n{result.get('markdown_table', '')}"
335	
336	    return ToolResult(
337	        success=True,
338	        tool_name="query_evidence_sql",
339	        markdown=_cite_inline(markdown, ev_ids),
340	        source_evidence_ids=ev_ids,
341	        statistics={"row_count": result["row_count"], "columns": result["columns"]},
342	    )
343	
344	
345	async def _wrap_statistical_summary(
346	    evidence_store: dict,
347	    data_points: list[dict],
348	    client: Any,
349	    **kwargs,
350	) -> ToolResult:
351	    """Wrapper for analysis_toolkit.statistical_summary().
352	
353	    Groups data by unit first to prevent mixing ng/L with $ with %.
354	    Uses the LARGEST unit group for the primary analysis.
355	    """
356	    from src.polaris_graph.tools.analysis_toolkit import statistical_summary
357	
358	    if not data_points:
359	        return ToolResult(
360	            success=False,
361	            tool_name="statistical_summary",
362	            markdown="No data points available for statistical summary.",
363	            error="Requires numeric data — run extract_numeric_data first",
364	        )
365	
366	    # Group by unit to prevent mixing ng/L with $ with %
367	    by_unit: dict[str, list] = {}
368	    for dp in data_points:
369	        unit = dp.get("unit", "unknown") or "unknown"
370	        by_unit.setdefault(unit, []).append(dp)
371	
372	    # Use the largest unit group (most meaningful)
373	    primary_unit = max(by_unit, key=lambda u: len(by_unit[u]))
374	    primary_data = by_unit[primary_unit]
375	
376	    stats = statistical_summary(primary_data)
377	    ev_ids = _extract_evidence_ids(primary_data)
378	
379	    if not stats.get("markdown_table"):
380	        return ToolResult(
381	            success=False,
382	            tool_name="statistical_summary",
383	            markdown="Could not compute statistics from provided data.",
384	            error="All values non-numeric",
385	        )
386	
387	    # Build cited markdown: table + insight paragraph with inline citations
388	    global_stats = stats.get("statistics", {})
389	    n = global_stats.get("n", 0)
390	    mean = global_stats.get("mean", 0)
391	    ci_lo = global_stats.get("ci_95_lower", 0)
392	    ci_hi = global_stats.get("ci_95_upper", 0)
393	
394	    # Insight paragraph carries the citations (not the table header)
395	    cite_str = "".join(f"[CITE:{eid}]" for eid in ev_ids[:10])
396	    insight_para = (
397	        f"\n\n**Summary (unit: {primary_unit}):** "
398	        f"Mean {mean:.2f} (95% CI: {ci_lo:.2f}–{ci_hi:.2f}) "
399	        f"across {n} data points from {len(set(dp.get('source_url', '') for dp in primary_data))} sources. "
400	        f"{cite_str}"
401	    )
402	
403	    return ToolResult(
404	        success=True,
405	        tool_name="statistical_summary",
406	        markdown=stats["markdown_table"] + insight_para,
407	        statistics=global_stats,
408	        source_evidence_ids=ev_ids,
409	        insights=stats.get("insights", []),
410	    )
411

413	async def _wrap_comparison_table(
414	    evidence_store: dict,
415	    data_points: list[dict],
416	    client: Any,
417	    **kwargs,
418	) -> ToolResult:
419	    """Wrapper for analysis_toolkit.build_comparison_table().
420	
421	    Groups by unit first, then caps to top 10 labels (columns) to
422	    prevent 78KB pivot tables. Picks the largest unit group.
423	    """
424	    from src.polaris_graph.tools.analysis_toolkit import build_comparison_table
425	
426	    if not data_points:
427	        return ToolResult(
428	            success=False,
429	            tool_name="comparison_table",
430	            markdown="No data points for comparison table.",
431	            error="Requires numeric data — run extract_numeric_data first",
432	        )
433	
434	    # Group by unit — compare within the same unit only
435	    by_unit: dict[str, list] = {}
436	    for dp in data_points:
437	        unit = dp.get("unit", "unknown") or "unknown"
438	        by_unit.setdefault(unit, []).append(dp)
439	
440	    primary_unit = max(by_unit, key=lambda u: len(by_unit[u]))
441	    unit_data = by_unit[primary_unit]
442	
443	    # Cap labels (columns) to top 10 by frequency
444	    label_counts: dict[str, int] = {}
445	    for dp in unit_data:
446	        label = dp.get("label", "")
447	        if label:
448	            label_counts[label] = label_counts.get(label, 0) + 1
449	    top_labels = sorted(label_counts, key=label_counts.get, reverse=True)[:10]
450	    filtered = [dp for dp in unit_data if dp.get("label", "") in top_labels]
451	
452	    if len(filtered) < 2:
453	        filtered = unit_data[:50]  # Fallback: just cap rows
454	
455	    # FIX-D2: Filter absurd numeric values before table construction
456	    _max_reasonable = float(os.getenv("PG_TABLE_MAX_VALUE", "1e9"))
457	    sane = []
458	    for dp in filtered:
459	        try:
460	            val = float(
461	                str(dp.get("value", 0)).replace(",", ""),
462	            )
463	            if abs(val) <= _max_reasonable:
464	                sane.append(dp)
465	        except (ValueError, TypeError):
466	            sane.append(dp)  # Keep non-numeric values
467	    if sane:
468	        filtered = sane
469	
470	    table_md = build_comparison_table(filtered)
471	    ev_ids = _extract_evidence_ids(filtered)
472	
473	    if not table_md or len(table_md) < 50:
474	        return ToolResult(
475	            success=False,
476	            tool_name="comparison_table",
477	            markdown="Insufficient data for a meaningful comparison table.",
478	            error="Need data from >= 2 sources",
479	        )
480	
481	    # Header with unit context
482	    header = f"**Comparison Table** (unit: {primary_unit}, {len(filtered)} data points)\n\n"
483	
484	    return ToolResult(
485	        success=True,
486	        tool_name="comparison_table",
487	        markdown=_cite_inline(header + table_md, ev_ids),
488	        source_evidence_ids=ev_ids,
489	        statistics={"unit": primary_unit, "data_points": len(filtered), "sources": len(set(dp.get("source_url", "") for dp in filtered))},
490	    )

586	    evidence_store: dict,
587	    data_points: list[dict],
588	    client: Any,
589	    **kwargs,
590	) -> ToolResult:
591	    """Wrapper for code_executor.generate_and_execute_analysis()."""
592	    from src.polaris_graph.tools.code_executor import generate_and_execute_analysis
593	
594	    if not client:
595	        return ToolResult(
596	            success=False,

598	            markdown="LLM client required for code generation.",
599	            error="No LLM client available",
600	        )
601	
602	    question = kwargs.get(
603	        "question",
604	        "Analyze key patterns and relationships in the data",
605	    )
606	    query = kwargs.get("research_context", "")
607	
608	    input_data = data_points[:30] if data_points else [
609	        {
610	            "statement": ev.get("statement", ""),
611	            "evidence_id": eid,
612	            "source_url": ev.get("source_url", ""),

621	        else [d.get("evidence_id", "") for d in input_data if d.get("evidence_id")]
622	    )
623	
624	    tool_timeout = int(os.getenv("PG_REACT_TOOL_TIMEOUT", "60"))

628	            generate_and_execute_analysis(

644	    if not result.get("success"):
645	        return ToolResult(
646	            success=False,
647	            tool_name="execute_python",
648	            markdown=f"Code execution failed: {result.get('error', 'unknown')[:200]}",

651	
652	    result_data = result.get("result", {})
653	    summary = (

657	    )

659	    insights = (

661	        if isinstance(result_data, dict)
662	        else []
663	    )
664	
665	    return ToolResult(
666	        success=True,
667	        tool_name="execute_python",
668	        markdown=_cite_inline(summary, ev_ids),
669	        charts=charts,
670	        source_evidence_ids=ev_ids,
671	        statistics=(
672	            result_data.get("statistics", {})
673	            if isinstance(result_data, dict)
674	            else {}
675	        ),
676	        insights=insights,
677	    )

1	"""
2	Structured pipeline tracer for polaris graph.
3	
4	Emits JSONL events to logs/pg_trace_{vector_id}.jsonl alongside
5	regular Python logging. Every node, LLM call, fetch, and quality
6	decision gets a machine-parseable trace event.
7	
8	OBS-1: Standalone tracing module with zero external dependencies.
9	Uses stdlib only: json, time, contextvars, dataclasses, pathlib.
10	"""
11	
12	import json
13	import logging
14	import os
15	import time
16	import uuid
17	from contextvars import ContextVar
18	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
19	from datetime import datetime, timezone
20	from pathlib import Path
21	from typing import Any, Optional
22	
23	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
24	
25	PG_TRACING_ENABLED = os.getenv("PG_TRACING_ENABLED", "1") == "1"
26	
27	# Thread-safe vector_id propagation
28	_current_vector_id: ContextVar[str] = ContextVar("pg_vector_id", default="unknown")
29	_current_tracer: ContextVar[Optional["PipelineTracer"]] = ContextVar("pg_tracer", default=None)
30	
31	
32	@dataclass
33	class TraceEvent:
34	    """A single structured trace event."""
35	
36	    timestamp: str
37	    vector_id: str
38	    node: str           # "plan", "search", "analyze", "verify", "evaluate", "synthesize"
39	    event_type: str     # "node_start", "node_end", "fetch", "llm_call", "quality_gate", etc.
40	    data: dict = field(default_factory=dict)
41	    duration_ms: float = 0.0
42	
43	
44	class PipelineTracer:
45	    """Structured JSONL tracer for pipeline observability.
46	
47	    Creates a JSONL file at logs/pg_trace_{vector_id}.jsonl and writes
48	    one event per line. Also keeps events in memory for summary.
49	
50	    Usage::
51	
52	        tracer = PipelineTracer("V001")
53	        tracer.node_start("plan", iteration=1)
54	        # ... do work ...
55	        tracer.node_end("plan", query_count=50)
56	        print(tracer.summary())
57	    """
58	
59	    def __init__(self, vector_id: str, output_dir: str = "logs"):
60	        self.vector_id = vector_id
61	        # W3.3: session_id disambiguates events when multiple runs append to
62	        # the same pg_trace_{vector_id}.jsonl. Readers can filter by sid to
63	        # isolate a single pipeline execution.
64	        self.session_id = uuid.uuid4().hex[:12]
65	        self._events: list[TraceEvent] = []
66	        self._node_timers: dict[str, float] = {}
67	        self._path = Path(output_dir) / f"pg_trace_{vector_id}.jsonl"
68	        self._path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
69	        _current_vector_id.set(vector_id)
70	        _current_tracer.set(self)
71	        # Mark the start of this session so a new file section is parseable.
72	        self._emit(
73	            event_type="session_start",
74	            node="pipeline",
75	            data={"session_id": self.session_id},
76	        )
77	
78	    def node_start(self, node: str, **data: Any) -> None:
79	        """Record node entry."""
80	        self._node_timers[node] = time.monotonic()
81	        self._emit("node_start", node, data)
82	
83	    def node_end(self, node: str, **data: Any) -> None:
84	        """Record node exit with wall-clock duration."""
85	        start = self._node_timers.pop(node, time.monotonic())
86	        duration_ms = (time.monotonic() - start) * 1000
87	        self._emit("node_end", node, {**data, "duration_ms": round(duration_ms, 1)})
88	
89	    def fetch(
90	        self,
91	        node: str,
92	        url: str,
93	        status: str,
94	        content_len: int = 0,
95	        duration_ms: float = 0,
96	        **data: Any,
97	    ) -> None:
98	        """Record a URL fetch attempt."""
99	        self._emit("fetch", node, {
100	            "url": url[:200],
101	            "status": status,
102	            "content_len": content_len,
103	            "duration_ms": round(duration_ms, 1),
104	            **data,
105	        })
106	
107	    def llm_call(
108	        self,
109	        node: str,
110	        call_type: str,
111	        input_tokens: int = 0,
112	        output_tokens: int = 0,
113	        duration_ms: float = 0,
114	        **data: Any,
115	    ) -> None:
116	        """Record an LLM API call."""
117	        self._emit("llm_call", node, {
118	            "call_type": call_type,
119	            "input_tokens": input_tokens,
120	            "output_tokens": output_tokens,
121	            "duration_ms": round(duration_ms, 1),
122	            **data,
123	        })
124	
125	    def evidence(self, node: str, action: str, count: int, **data: Any) -> None:
126	        """Record evidence flow (extracted, filtered, deduped, accumulated)."""
127	        self._emit("evidence", node, {"action": action, "count": count, **data})
128	
129	    def quality_gate(self, node: str, gate: str, passed: bool, **data: Any) -> None:
130	        """Record a quality gate check."""
131	        self._emit("quality_gate", node, {"gate": gate, "passed": passed, **data})
132	
133	    def query(self, node: str, action: str, queries: "list[str] | int", **data: Any) -> None:
134	        """Record query generation/amplification."""
135	        count = queries if isinstance(queries, int) else len(queries)
136	        self._emit("query", node, {"action": action, "count": count, **data})
137	
138	    def search_result(
139	        self,
140	        node: str,
141	        engine: str,
142	        query: str,
143	        result_count: int,
144	        **data: Any,
145	    ) -> None:
146	        """Record search results per query."""
147	        self._emit("search_result", node, {
148	            "engine": engine,
149	            "query": query,
150	            "result_count": result_count,
151	            **data,
152	        })
153	
154	    def reasoning_capture(
155	        self, node: str, call_type: str, reasoning_text: str,
156	        prompt_excerpt: str = "", **data: Any,
157	    ) -> None:
158	        """Record LLM reasoning content from any call."""
159	        self._emit("reasoning_capture", node, {
160	            "call_type": call_type,
161	            "reasoning_text": reasoning_text[:50000],
162	            "chars": len(reasoning_text or ""),
163	            "prompt_excerpt": prompt_excerpt[:500],
164	            **data,
165	        })
166	
167	    def storm_transcript(
168	        self, persona: str, round_num: int,
169	        question: str, answer: str, sources: list[str],
170	        key_findings: list[str], **data: Any,
171	    ) -> None:
172	        """Record a full STORM interview Q&A round."""
173	        self._emit("storm_transcript", "storm_interviews", {
174	            "persona": persona,
175	            "round": round_num,
176	            "question": question[:2000],
177	            "answer": answer[:5000],
178	            "sources": sources[:20],
179	            "key_findings": key_findings[:10],
180	            **data,
181	        })
182	
183	    def iteration_decision(
184	        self, iteration: int, decision: str, rationale: dict,
185	        **data: Any,
186	    ) -> None:
187	        """Record an iteration routing decision with full rationale."""
188	        self._emit("iteration_decision", "evaluate", {
189	            "iteration": iteration,
190	            "decision": decision,
191	            "rationale": rationale,
192	            **data,
193	        })
194	
195	    def summary(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
196	        """Return a summary of all trace events for state storage."""
197	        node_durations: dict[str, float] = {}
198	        event_counts: dict[str, int] = {}
199	        for e in self._events:
200	            event_counts[e.event_type] = event_counts.get(e.event_type, 0) + 1
201	            if e.event_type == "node_end" and "duration_ms" in e.data:
202	                node_durations[e.node] = e.data["duration_ms"]
203	
204	        return {
205	            "total_events": len(self._events),
206	            "nodes": list(set(e.node for e in self._events)),
207	            "event_counts": event_counts,
208	            "node_durations_ms": node_durations,
209	            "trace_file": str(self._path),
210	        }
211	
212	    def log_event(
213	        self, event_type: str, data: dict | None = None, node: str = "pipeline"
214	    ) -> None:
215	        """Generic event logging — public API for components like SmartArt."""
216	        self._emit(event_type=event_type, node=node, data=data or {})
217	
218	    def _emit(self, event_type: str, node: str, data: dict) -> None:
219	        """Write a trace event to JSONL and memory."""
220	        if not PG_TRACING_ENABLED:
221	            return
222	        event = TraceEvent(
223	            timestamp=datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
224	            vector_id=self.vector_id,
225	            node=node,
226	            event_type=event_type,
227	            data=data,
228	        )
229	        self._events.append(event)
230	        # Write to JSONL file
231	        try:
232	            with open(self._path, "a", encoding="utf-8") as f:
233	                f.write(json.dumps({
234	                    "ts": event.timestamp,
235	                    "vid": event.vector_id,
236	                    # W3.3: tag every event with the session_id so readers
237	                    # can filter to a single pipeline run in a shared trace.
238	                    "sid": self.session_id,
239	                    "node": event.node,
240	                    "type": event.event_type,
241	                    **event.data,
242	                }, default=str) + "\n")
243	        except Exception as exc:
244	            logger.warning("[polaris graph] Tracing write failed: %s", exc)
245	
246	
247	def get_tracer() -> Optional[PipelineTracer]:
248	    """Get the current pipeline tracer (if any)."""
249	    return _current_tracer.get(None)

1	"""I-arch-001b — synthesize a v30.1 per_query_report_contract from a v6 template.
2	
3	Input: v6 template JSON (config/v6_templates/<template_id>.json), query_slug.
4	Output: {query_slug: <v30.1 contract dict>} ready to merge into a scope
5	template's `per_query_report_contract` key, where M-55 frame_compiler and
6	load_report_contract_for_slug both consume it.
7	
8	Per Codex APPROVE iter 5: every entity carries a concrete http(s) url_pattern
9	from T1 + a stable anchor; this satisfies M-56's `_fetch_url_pattern()` which
10	treats the value as a literal URL, not a regex.
11	"""
12	
13	from __future__ import annotations
14	
15	import hashlib
16	from typing import Any
17	
18	# Per-template defaults for the type discriminator + identifier field names.
19	# Codex iter-2: rolled clinical→drug/condition into a generic "frame" entity-
20	# type so the v30 compiler doesn't try to dispatch on type-specific paths
21	# that don't exist for synthesized contracts.
22	_TYPE_FOR_TEMPLATE: dict[str, str] = {
23	    "clinical": "drug",
24	    "policy": "policy",
25	    "tech": "policy",
26	    "due_diligence": "policy",
27	    "ai_sovereignty": "policy",
28	    "canada_us": "policy",
29	    "workforce": "policy",
30	    "custom": "policy",
31	}
32	
33	_REQUIRED_FIELDS_FOR_TEMPLATE: dict[str, list[str]] = {
34	    "clinical": ["rxcui", "drugbank_id", "icd_10"],
35	    "policy": ["jurisdiction", "policy_id", "year"],
36	    "tech": ["jurisdiction", "technology_id", "year"],
37	    "due_diligence": ["entity_id", "jurisdiction", "year"],
38	    "ai_sovereignty": ["bill_id", "jurisdiction", "year"],
39	    "canada_us": ["jurisdiction", "treaty_id", "year"],
40	    "workforce": ["jurisdiction", "indicator_id", "year"],
41	    "custom": ["jurisdiction", "topic_id", "year"],
42	}
43	
44	# Last-resort URL when a template has empty T1. Per Codex iter-5 P2 accepted-
45	# remaining: this is acceptable for v1 (all 8 current templates have T1);
46	# future templates should fail-loud, deferred to follow-up.
47	_EMPTY_T1_FALLBACK = "https://www.canada.ca/en/research.html"
48	
49	
50	def _anchor_for(template_id: str, frame_id: str, query_slug: str) -> str:
51	    """Stable, unique anchor: <template>:<frame>:<slug_prefix>:<sha256_8>.
52	
53	    Used as a secondary stable id (not the retrieval locator — url_pattern is).
54	    Collision-safe for any realistic curator workload (2^32 combinations).
55	    """
56	    h = hashlib.sha256(
57	        f"{template_id}|{frame_id}|{query_slug}".encode("utf-8")
58	    ).hexdigest()[:8]
59	    return f"{template_id}:{frame_id}:{query_slug[:40]}:{h}"
60	
61	
62	def _concrete_url_for_entity(frame_idx: int, source_tiers: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
63	    """Concrete http(s) URL for this entity, rotated through T1 by frame index.
64	
65	    M-56's `_fetch_url_pattern()` treats this value as a literal URL, NOT a
66	    regex (per Codex iter-4 P1 verification at frame_fetcher.py:780,1046).
67	    Returning a regex would fail to fetch.
68	
69	    Rotation: entity[i] uses T1[i % len(T1)]. Different frames retrieve
70	    different roots; strict_verify later filters per-frame relevance.
71	    """
72	    t1 = source_tiers.get("T1") or []
73	    if t1:
74	        return t1[frame_idx % len(t1)]
75	    return _EMPTY_T1_FALLBACK
76	
77	
78	def build_v30_contract(
79	    v6_template: dict[str, Any],
80	    query_slug: str,
81	    question: str | None = None,
82	) -> dict[str, Any]:
83	    """Synthesize a v30.1 per_query_report_contract.{query_slug} dict from a v6 template.
84	
85	    Args:
86	        v6_template: loaded v6 template JSON (config/v6_templates/<id>.json)
87	        query_slug: pipeline-A URL-safe slug (e.g. "clinical_glp1_t2dm")
88	        question: reserved for future question-aware seeding; iter-1 implementation
89	                  uses frame_id as entity_id basis.
90	
91	    Returns:
92	        {query_slug: <contract>} where <contract> has schema_version="v30.1",
93	        required_entities, rendering_slots (dict-keyed by slot_id), and
94	        section_order. Round-trips clean via load_report_contract_for_slug.
95	    """
96	    del question  # reserved; suppress unused-arg warning
97	    template_id = v6_template.get("template_id", "")
98	    frame_manifest = v6_template.get("frame_manifest") or []
99	    source_tiers = v6_template.get("source_tiers") or {}
100	
101	    entity_type = _TYPE_FOR_TEMPLATE.get(template_id, "policy")
102	    required_fields = _REQUIRED_FIELDS_FOR_TEMPLATE.get(
103	        template_id, ["jurisdiction", "year"]
104	    )
105	
106	    required_entities: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
107	    rendering_slots: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = {}
108	    sections_seen: list[str] = []  # ordered, deduped
109	
110	    for idx, frame in enumerate(frame_manifest):
111	        frame_id = frame.get("frame_id", f"frame_{idx}")
112	        frame_name = frame.get("frame_name", frame_id.replace("_", " ").title())
113	        slot_id = f"{frame_id}_slot"
114	        section = _section_for_frame(template_id, frame_id, idx)
115	        if section not in sections_seen:
116	            sections_seen.append(section)
117	
118	        rendering_slots[slot_id] = {
119	            "section": section,
120	            "subsection_title": frame_name,
121	            "ordering": idx + 1,
122	            "required": True,
123	        }
124	        required_entities.append({
125	            "id": f"{frame_id}_entity",
126	            "type": entity_type,
127	            "required_fields": list(required_fields),
128	            "min_fields_for_completion": 1,
129	            "rendering_slot": slot_id,
130	            "url_pattern": _concrete_url_for_entity(idx, source_tiers),
131	            "anchor": _anchor_for(template_id, frame_id, query_slug),
132	        })
133	
134	    contract = {
135	        "schema_version": "v30.1",
136	        "required_entities": required_entities,
137	        "rendering_slots": rendering_slots,
138	        "section_order": sections_seen,
139	    }
140	    return {query_slug: contract}
141	
142	
143	def _section_for_frame(template_id: str, frame_id: str, idx: int) -> str:
144	    """Group frames into 2-4 sections per template. Mechanical grouping; per-
145	    template overrides can be added later via report_contract_override blocks.
146	    """
147	    # Per Codex iter-5 P2: section grouping is left mechanical-but-stable;
148	    # template-specific overrides are a follow-up.
149	    if template_id == "clinical":
150	        section_map = {
151	            "efficacy": "Efficacy",
152	            "safety": "Safety",
153	            "labelling": "Regulatory",
154	            "post_market": "Post-market",
155	            "subgroup": "Subgroups",
156	        }
157	    else:
158	        # Policy templates default: split into Background / Findings / Implications.
159	        if idx < 2:
160	            return "Background"
161	        if idx < 4:
162	            return "Findings"
163	        return "Implications"
164	    return section_map.get(frame_id, "Other")

1	"""
2	Mesh claim extraction — L2 write path.
3	
4	Takes a source_page row + its body text, calls the LLM to extract
5	atomic facts, filters garbage, computes body-relative char spans,
6	assigns tiers, and inserts the result as `claims` rows via MeshStore.
7	
8	Design (per advisor CP-A):
9	
10	  - REUSE the production schemas (`AtomicFact`, `SourceAnalysis`,
11	    `SourceAnalysisBatch`) from `src.polaris_graph.schemas`. Those have
12	    40+ runs of Qwen field-name normalization baked into their
13	    `@model_validator(mode="before")` methods — rewriting them would
14	    introduce silent parsing bugs.
15	
16	  - REUSE the `ANALYSIS_SYSTEM` prompt from
17	    `src.polaris_graph.agents.analyzer`. Duplicating it creates drift
18	    between copies and breaks one while fixing the other.
19	
20	  - SPLIT into two layers for testability:
21	      `_parse_batch_to_claims(parsed, source_body, ...) -> list[dict]`
22	          Pure function. No I/O. Takes a SourceAnalysisBatch, returns
23	          a list of claim dicts ready for `store.insert_claim()`.
24	      `extract_claims_from_source(client, store, ...) -> list[str]`
25	          Orchestrator. Reads body from disk, calls LLM, parses,
26	          inserts, returns claim IDs.
27	    The parser covers 80% of the test surface without any LLM mocking.
28	
29	  - char_start / char_end are relative to the source BODY (the text
30	    after ingest.py's markdown header). Downstream drill-down code
31	    must read the body via `ingest.read_source_text`, not raw file
32	    reads. See the comment in `ingest.py` near `_HEADER_TERMINATOR`.
33	
34	  - Unverifiable quotes are NOT dropped — they get a sentinel span
35	    `char_start=0, char_end=1` and a BRONZE tier. Dropping claims
36	    because the LLM paraphrased slightly would replicate the
37	    "NLI too strict for niche domains" failure mode from memory.
38	
39	  - Tier assignment: 3 signals for v1.
40	      GOLD   = relevance >= 0.7 AND source_quality >= 0.6 AND verified
41	      SILVER = relevance >= 0.4 (if verified) OR relevance >= 0.5 (unverified)
42	      BRONZE = everything else
43	    The full 5-signal production tier logic is deferred to Unit 4
44	    where edge discovery provides corroboration-count inputs.
45	
46	  - has_numeric detection runs against direct_quote — a cheap regex
47	    catching CIs, p-values, effect sizes, sample sizes, percentages.
48	
49	  - Filters ported from analyzer.py::_analyze_batch:
50	      - statement shorter than 10 chars → skip
51	      - quote with fewer than PG_MIN_QUOTE_WORDS words → skip
52	      - URL fragment in quote → skip
53	      - cookie / consent boilerplate in quote → skip
54	
55	  - NOT ported (production-pipeline specific):
56	      - fetch_method propagation
57	      - citation_count propagation
58	      - STORM perspective_source tagging
59	      - source_content_store writes
60	"""
61	
62	from __future__ import annotations
63	
64	import logging
65	import os
66	import re
67	from typing import Any, Protocol
68	
69	import numpy as np
70	
71	from src.polaris_graph.agents.analyzer import ANALYSIS_SYSTEM
72	from src.polaris_graph.schemas import SourceAnalysisBatch
73	
74	from .entity import DisambigClient, canonicalize_entities_for_claim
75	from .ingest import read_source_text
76	from .store import EMBEDDING_DIM, MeshStore, MeshStoreError
77	
78	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
79	
80	
81	# ───── mesh-side system prompt extension ─────
82	#
83	# CP-A lock (c2): the mesh pipeline MUST NOT modify analyzer.py's
84	# ANALYSIS_SYSTEM, which is shared with 40+ production runs. Instead,
85	# we wrap it with a mesh-specific suffix that asks the LLM to populate
86	# the new `entities` field on each atomic_fact. The Pydantic schema's
87	# `normalize_field_names` validator handles the backward-compat path
88	# (empty list default) if the LLM ignores this suffix.
89	
90	MESH_SYSTEM = ANALYSIS_SYSTEM + (
91	    "\n\nMESH OVERRIDE — QUOTE LENGTH:\n"
92	    "IMPORTANT: Override rule 10 above about 150-character quote limits.\n"
93	    "For this task, the `direct_quote` field MUST contain the FULL sentence "
94	    "or clause from the source text — minimum 15 words, up to 300 characters. "
95	    "Do NOT truncate to a short key phrase. Copy the complete sentence that "
96	    "contains the finding, including surrounding context. Longer quotes are "
97	    "strongly preferred over short fragments.\n"
98	    "\n"
99	    "MESH EXTENSION — ENTITY EXTRACTION:\n"
100	    "For each atomic_fact you emit, populate the `entities` field with a "
101	    "list of key named entities mentioned in the fact. Valid entity types:\n"
102	    "  - Chemical compounds (e.g., \"PFOS\", \"PFOA\", \"perfluorooctane sulfonate\")\n"
103	    "  - Methods / techniques (e.g., \"GAC\", \"ion exchange\", \"reverse osmosis\")\n"
104	    "  - Organizations (e.g., \"EPA\", \"Water Research Foundation\")\n"
105	    "  - People (e.g., \"Dr. John Smith\")\n"
106	    "  - Metrics (e.g., \"95% CI\", \"p < 0.05\")\n"
107	    "\n"
108	    "Rules:\n"
109	    "  - Emit 1-5 entities per fact. Prefer canonical / well-known forms.\n"
110	    "  - If no named entity is present, return an empty list.\n"
111	    "  - Do NOT paraphrase — use the exact term as it appears in the quote.\n"
112	    "  - Do NOT emit long phrases; entity names should be short (≤ 80 chars).\n"
113	)
114	
115	
116	# ───── constants (mirroring analyzer.py) ─────
117	
118	PG_MIN_QUOTE_WORDS = int(os.getenv("PG_MIN_QUOTE_WORDS", "5"))
119	PG_MIN_STATEMENT_LEN = int(os.getenv("PG_MIN_STATEMENT_LEN", "10"))
120	PG_CONTENT_PER_SOURCE = int(os.getenv("PG_CONTENT_PER_SOURCE", "25000"))
121	PG_EXTRACTION_MAX_TOKENS = int(os.getenv("PG_EXTRACTION_MAX_TOKENS", "16384"))
122	PG_EXTRACTION_TIMEOUT = int(os.getenv("PG_EXTRACTION_TIMEOUT", "180"))
123	
124	# Sentinel char span for claims whose direct_quote could not be located
125	# in the source body. Store's CHECK constraint requires char_end > char_start,
126	# so (0, 1) is the minimum legal span.
127	UNVERIFIED_CHAR_START = 0
128	UNVERIFIED_CHAR_END = 1
129	
130	# Regex patterns for the URL / cookie / nav filters ported from
131	# analyzer.py::_analyze_batch (lines 2063-2075).
132	_URL_MARKERS = ("http://", "https://", ".com/", ".gov/", ".org/", ".edu/", "www.")
133	_COOKIE_MARKERS = (
134	    "cookie", "advertising", "track the user", "consent", "privacy policy",
135	)
136	
137	# has_numeric detector: catches any claim containing quantitative data.
138	# FIX-C5: the original pattern only caught formal stats (95% CI, p<0.05).
139	# Real claims contain "85%", "$0.15", "42 measurements", "3-5 kWh".
140	_NUMERIC_PATTERN = re.compile(
141	    r"(?:"
142	    r"\d+\s*%"                                     # any percentage: 85%, 40-60%
143	    r"|[$]\s*\d"                                    # dollar amounts: $0.15
144	    r"|\d+(?:\.\d+)?\s*(?:kWh|psi|mg/L|ng/L|ppm|ppb|ppt|Daltons)"  # units
145	    r"|\d+\s*(?:times|x)\s+more"                   # comparative: 2-3 times more
146	    r"|95\s*%\s*CI|p\s*[<>=]\s*0?\.\d|n\s*=\s*\d"  # formal stats
147	    r"|±\s*\d"                                      # plus/minus
148	    r"|(?:OR|HR|RR|SMD|WMD|MD)\s*[:=]\s*[-\d]"     # effect sizes
149	    r"|\d+-\d+\s*%"                                 # ranges: 40-60%
150	    r"|\d+[\s\-]+(?:month|year|day|hour|minute)s?"   # durations: 12-month, 6 months
151	    r")",
152	    re.IGNORECASE,
153	)
154	
155	
156	# ───── client protocol (for typing, not enforcement) ─────
157	
158	class _LLMClient(Protocol):
159	    """Minimal protocol — matches `OpenRouterClient.generate_structured`.
160	
161	    Tests inject a mock that implements just this method; production code
162	    passes the real `OpenRouterClient` instance.
163	    """
164	
165	    async def generate_structured(
166	        self,
167	        *,
168	        prompt: str,
169	        schema: Any,
170	        system: str,
171	        max_tokens: int,
172	        timeout: int,
173	        reasoning_enabled: bool,
174	    ) -> Any: ...  # returns a SourceAnalysisBatch
175	
176	
177	# ───── extraction result dataclass ─────
178	
179	class ExtractionResult:
180	    """Bundle returned from extract_claims_from_source.
181	
182	    Attributes:
183	        inserted_claim_ids: list of claim IDs written to the store
184	        skipped: counts per reason (for observability)
185	        total_facts_seen: how many atomic_facts the LLM returned
186	    """
187	    __slots__ = ("inserted_claim_ids", "skipped", "total_facts_seen", "rejected_quotes")
188	
189	    def __init__(self) -> None:
190	        self.inserted_claim_ids: list[str] = []
191	        self.skipped: dict[str, int] = {
192	            "short_statement": 0,
193	            "short_quote": 0,
194	            "url_fragment": 0,
195	            "cookie_text": 0,
196	        }
197	        self.total_facts_seen: int = 0
198	        self.rejected_quotes: list[tuple[int, str]] = []
199	
200	    def as_dict(self) -> dict:
201	        return {
202	            "inserted_count": len(self.inserted_claim_ids),
203	            "inserted_claim_ids": list(self.inserted_claim_ids),
204	            "skipped": dict(self.skipped),
205	            "total_facts_seen": self.total_facts_seen,
206	        }
207	
208	
209	# ───── pure parser (no I/O, no store, no LLM) ─────
210	
211	def _parse_batch_to_claims(
212	    *,
213	    parsed: SourceAnalysisBatch,
214	    source_body: str,
215	    source_url: str,
216	) -> tuple[list[dict], ExtractionResult]:
217	    """
218	    Transform a SourceAnalysisBatch into a list of claim dicts ready for
219	    `store.insert_claim()`.
220	
221	    Pure function: no file I/O, no LLM calls, no database writes. This
222	    is 80% of the test surface — exercise it directly with mocked
223	    `SourceAnalysisBatch` inputs, no client mocking needed.
224	
225	    Returns:
226	        (claim_dicts, result)
227	        - claim_dicts: list of kwargs-style dicts, each directly passable
228	          to `store.insert_claim(**claim_dict)`
229	        - result: ExtractionResult with skip counts and total_facts_seen
230	
231	    The caller is responsible for matching the right SourceAnalysis to
232	    the right source_page (this function operates on a single source at
233	    a time — pass the parsed batch but only the analyses matching
234	    `source_url` are consumed).
235	    """
236	    result = ExtractionResult()
237	    claim_dicts: list[dict] = []
238	
239	    matching_analyses = [
240	        a for a in parsed.analyses if a.source_url == source_url
241	    ]
242	    if not matching_analyses:
243	        logger.warning(
244	            "_parse_batch_to_claims: no analyses matched source_url=%s "
245	            "(found %d analyses for other URLs)",
246	            source_url[:80], len(parsed.analyses),
247	        )
248	        return claim_dicts, result
249	
250	    # Lowercase the source body ONCE for all quote lookups
251	    body_lower = source_body.lower()
252	
253	    for analysis in matching_analyses:
254	        source_quality = float(analysis.source_quality or 0.0)
255	
256	        for fact in analysis.atomic_facts:
257	            result.total_facts_seen += 1
258	
259	            # ── Filter 1: statement too short ──
260	            statement = (fact.statement or "").strip()
261	            if len(statement) < PG_MIN_STATEMENT_LEN:
262	                result.skipped["short_statement"] += 1
263	                continue
264	
265	            # ── Filter 2: quote too short ──
266	            quote = (fact.direct_quote or "").strip()
267	            if len(quote.split()) < PG_MIN_QUOTE_WORDS:
268	                result.skipped["short_quote"] += 1
269	                result.rejected_quotes.append(
270	                    (len(quote.split()), quote[:120]),
271	                )
272	                continue
273	
274	            # ── Filter 3: URL fragment ──
275	            quote_lower = quote.lower()
276	            if any(m in quote_lower for m in _URL_MARKERS):
277	                result.skipped["url_fragment"] += 1
278	                continue
279	
280	            # ── Filter 4: cookie / consent boilerplate ──
281	            if any(m in quote_lower for m in _COOKIE_MARKERS):
282	                result.skipped["cookie_text"] += 1
283	                continue
284	
285	            # ── Char-span lookup against the BODY (not the LLM input) ──
286	            char_start, char_end, verified = _locate_quote(quote, body_lower, source_body)
287	
288	            # ── has_numeric detection ──
289	            has_numeric = bool(_NUMERIC_PATTERN.search(quote))
290	
291	            # ── Tier assignment (3 signals for v1) ──
292	            relevance = _clamp01(fact.relevance_score)
293	            tier = _assign_tier(
294	                relevance=relevance,
295	                source_quality=source_quality,
296	                verified=verified,
297	            )
298	
299	            # Collect entity surface forms from the AtomicFact. Sanitize
300	            # here (strip, drop empties, drop over-long entries) so the
301	            # downstream canonicalizer always sees clean input.
302	            raw_ents = getattr(fact, "entities", None) or []
303	            surface_forms: list[str] = []
304	            for ent in raw_ents:
305	                ent_s = str(ent or "").strip()
306	                if ent_s and len(ent_s) <= 80:
307	                    surface_forms.append(ent_s)
308	
309	            claim_dicts.append({
310	                "statement": statement,
311	                "direct_quote": quote[:500],  # mirror analyzer's cap
312	                "char_start": char_start,
313	                "char_end": char_end,
314	                "tier": tier,
315	                "relevance_score": relevance,
316	                "has_numeric": has_numeric,
317	                "entities": surface_forms,
318	            })
319	
320	    return claim_dicts, result
321	
322	
323	# ───── orchestrator (I/O + LLM) ─────
324	
325	async def extract_claims_from_source(
326	    *,
327	    client: _LLMClient,
328	    store: MeshStore,
329	    workspace_id: str,
330	    source_page_id: str,
331	    query: str,
332	    disambig_client: DisambigClient | None = None,
333	) -> ExtractionResult:
334	    """
335	    Full L2 write path for a single source_page.
336	
337	    Reads the source body from disk, calls the LLM with the production
338	    ANALYSIS_SYSTEM prompt (wrapped by MESH_SYSTEM), parses the response,
339	    inserts the result as claim rows via `store.insert_claim`, and
340	    canonicalizes each claim's entity surface forms through
341	    `entity.canonicalize_entities_for_claim`. The whole insert phase
342	    runs inside a single transaction so a partial parser failure rolls
343	    back cleanly and does not double-count the workspace claim counter.
344	
345	    `disambig_client` is optional: when None, entity canonicalization
346	    falls through step 4 (no LLM disambig in the 0.80-0.92 cosine zone)
347	    and just inserts new quarantined entities. Production callers can
348	    pass the same client they use for extraction if they want the
349	    disambig step to run; test callers can leave it None.
350	
351	    Returns an ExtractionResult with the inserted claim IDs and skip
352	    counts.
353	    """
354	    src = store.get_source(source_page_id)
355	    if src is None:
356	        raise MeshStoreError(f"Source not found: {source_page_id}")
357	    if src["workspace_id"] != workspace_id:
358	        raise MeshStoreError(
359	            f"Source {source_page_id} belongs to workspace "
360	            f"{src['workspace_id']}, not {workspace_id}"
361	        )
362	
363	    # Resolve the on-disk path and read the body (header-stripped)
364	    md_path = store.workspace_dir / src["filepath"]
365	    source_body = read_source_text(md_path)
366	    if not source_body.strip():
367	        raise MeshStoreError(
368	            f"Source body is empty for {source_page_id} at {md_path}"
369	        )
370	
371	    # Cap the content sent to the LLM (matches analyzer.py behavior)
372	    llm_content = source_body[:PG_CONTENT_PER_SOURCE]
373	    source_url = src.get("url") or f"mesh://{source_page_id}"
374	    source_title = src.get("title") or ""
375	    source_type = src.get("kind") or "web"
376	
377	    prompt = (
378	        f"Research question: {query}\n\n"
379	        f"Analyze the following source and extract the TOP 8-15 MOST "
380	        f"relevant atomic facts. Focus on the most specific, unique, "
381	        f"and directly relevant numbers, dates, measurements, claims, "
382	        f"findings, regulations, and standards. Quality over quantity.\n\n"
383	        f"Source URL: {source_url}\n"
384	        f"Source title: {source_title}\n"
385	        f"Source type: {source_type}\n"
386	        f"Content:\n{llm_content}\n"
387	        f"---"
388	    )
389	
390	    parsed = await client.generate_structured(
391	        prompt=prompt,
392	        schema=SourceAnalysisBatch,
393	        system=MESH_SYSTEM,
394	        max_tokens=PG_EXTRACTION_MAX_TOKENS,
395	        timeout=PG_EXTRACTION_TIMEOUT,
396	        reasoning_enabled=False,
397	    )
398	
399	    # Parse (pure function — no I/O)
400	    claim_dicts, result = _parse_batch_to_claims(
401	        parsed=parsed,
402	        source_body=source_body,
403	        source_url=source_url,
404	    )
405	
406	    # Embed every surviving claim statement BEFORE opening the transaction.
407	    # Embedding is slow (GPU/CPU inference) and we don't want to hold a
408	    # DB write lock while it runs. The embed call is idempotent and
409	    # stateless — if the transaction later fails, we throw the vectors
410	    # away without corruption. Atomicity is preserved because the vector
411	    # insert inside `store.insert_claim` (via `_insert_vector`) is in
412	    # the same transaction as the row insert.
413	    embeddings: list[np.ndarray] = []
414	    surface_embeddings: dict[str, np.ndarray] = {}
415	    if claim_dicts:
416	        try:
417	            from src.utils.embedding_service import embed_texts
418	        except ImportError as exc:
419	            raise MeshStoreError(
420	                "src.utils.embedding_service is required for claim extraction "
421	                f"({exc}). Install dependencies or run tests with a mock."
422	            ) from exc
423	        raw_vecs = embed_texts([c["statement"] for c in claim_dicts])
424	        for vec in raw_vecs:
425	            arr = np.asarray(vec, dtype=np.float32)
426	            if arr.shape != (EMBEDDING_DIM,):
427	                raise MeshStoreError(
428	                    f"Embedding service returned shape {arr.shape}, "
429	                    f"expected ({EMBEDDING_DIM},). Model change detected — "
430	                    f"update schema.VECTOR_DDL and EMBEDDING_DIM to match."
431	                )
432	            embeddings.append(arr)
433	
434	        # Collect the unique set of entity surface forms across every
435	        # claim, then batch-embed them all in one embed_texts() call.
436	        # Amortizes the model forward-pass overhead across all entities
437	        # in this source. Falls through gracefully if no claims have
438	        # entities (empty set → no embed call).
439	        unique_surfaces: list[str] = sorted({
440	            s for c in claim_dicts for s in c.get("entities", [])
441	        })
442	        if unique_surfaces:
443	            raw_surface_vecs = embed_texts(unique_surfaces)
444	            for surf, vec in zip(unique_surfaces, raw_surface_vecs):
445	                arr = np.asarray(vec, dtype=np.float32)
446	                if arr.shape != (EMBEDDING_DIM,):
447	                    raise MeshStoreError(
448	                        f"Embedding service returned shape {arr.shape} "
449	                        f"for entity surface, expected ({EMBEDDING_DIM},)."
450	                    )
451	                surface_embeddings[surf] = arr
452	
453	    # Insert everything atomically — claims + vectors + entities in one tx
454	    with store.transaction():
455	        for claim_kwargs, emb in zip(claim_dicts, embeddings):
456	            # The parser attaches an "entities" key that insert_claim
457	            # does not accept. Pop it before the insert, then run the
458	            # canonicalization pass inside the same transaction so a
459	            # mid-batch failure rolls back claim + entity links together.
460	            entities_for_claim = claim_kwargs.pop("entities", [])
461	
462	            clm_id = store.insert_claim(
463	                workspace_id=workspace_id,
464	                source_page_id=source_page_id,
465	                embedding=emb,
466	                **claim_kwargs,
467	            )
468	            result.inserted_claim_ids.append(clm_id)
469	
470	            if entities_for_claim:
471	                await canonicalize_entities_for_claim(
472	                    store,
473	                    workspace_id=workspace_id,
474	                    claim_id=clm_id,
475	                    surface_forms=entities_for_claim,
476	                    disambig_client=disambig_client,
477	                    embeddings=surface_embeddings,
478	                )
479	
480	    logger.info(
481	        "extract_claims_from_source %s: %d inserted, %d total seen, skipped=%s",
482	        source_page_id, len(result.inserted_claim_ids),
483	        result.total_facts_seen, result.skipped,
484	    )
485	    return result
486	
487	
488	# ───── helpers ─────
489	
490	def _clamp01(v: float | None) -> float:
491	    if v is None:
492	        return 0.1
493	    try:
494	        f = float(v)
495	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
496	        return 0.1
497	    if f < 0.0:
498	        return 0.0
499	    if f > 1.0:
500	        return 1.0
501	    return f
502	
503	
504	def _locate_quote(
505	    quote: str,
506	    body_lower: str,
507	    body: str,
508	) -> tuple[int, int, bool]:
509	    """
510	    Find `quote` in `body_lower` (the lowercased body). Returns
511	    (char_start, char_end, verified). If not found, returns the
512	    sentinel (UNVERIFIED_CHAR_START, UNVERIFIED_CHAR_END, False).
513	
514	    We search with the first 50 chars of the quote (matching the
515	    production heuristic in analyzer.py:2126) to tolerate minor
516	    trailing drift (punctuation, whitespace, LLM paraphrase).
517	    """
518	    quote_lower = quote.lower().strip()
519	    if not quote_lower or not body_lower:
520	        return UNVERIFIED_CHAR_START, UNVERIFIED_CHAR_END, False
521	
522	    # Try the first 50-char prefix first (tolerates trailing paraphrase)
523	    prefix = quote_lower[:50]
524	    start = body_lower.find(prefix)
525	    if start < 0:
526	        return UNVERIFIED_CHAR_START, UNVERIFIED_CHAR_END, False
527	
528	    # Try to locate the full quote starting from this position
529	    full_pos = body_lower.find(quote_lower, start)
530	    if full_pos >= 0:
531	        end = full_pos + len(quote_lower)
532	    else:
533	        end = start + len(prefix)
534	
535	    # Sanity check: both within body bounds
536	    if start >= len(body) or end > len(body) or end <= start:
537	        return UNVERIFIED_CHAR_START, UNVERIFIED_CHAR_END, False
538	
539	    return start, end, True
540	
541	
542	def _assign_tier(
543	    *,
544	    relevance: float,
545	    source_quality: float,
546	    verified: bool,
547	) -> str:
548	    """
549	    v1 tier assignment using two signals: relevance + quote verification.
550	
551	    FIX-S1: Removed source_quality gate from GOLD. The LLM-assigned
552	    source_quality was deterministic by source (GAC/RO=0.8, AIX/NF=0.5),
553	    causing ALL claims from ion_exchange and nanofiltration to get SILVER
554	    regardless of relevance or verification quality. GOLD should mean
555	    "high relevance + verified quote", not "the LLM rated this source
556	    highly". Source quality is still available as a signal but no longer
557	    gates tier assignment.
558	
559	        GOLD   = relevance >= 0.7 AND verified
560	        SILVER = (relevance >= 0.4 AND verified) OR (relevance >= 0.5)
561	        BRONZE = everything else
562	
563	    Unverifiable but highly relevant claims still get SILVER — we
564	    don't want to bury a 0.9-relevance claim as BRONZE just because
565	    the LLM paraphrased the quote slightly and the body-search missed.
566	    """
567	    if verified and relevance >= 0.7:
568	        return "GOLD"
569	    if (verified and relevance >= 0.4) or relevance >= 0.5:
570	        return "SILVER"
571	    return "BRONZE"
572
