1	"""
2	Wiki Source Quality — enhanced scoring with study design, retraction check,
3	content completeness, and author authority signals.
4	
5	Wraps the existing src/utils/source_quality.py scorer and adds signals
6	from S2 publicationTypes, OpenAlex is_retracted/2yr_mean_citedness, and
7	content analysis.
8	
9	All new signals come from APIs already called — just need additional fields
10	requested in the API queries.
11	"""
12	
13	import logging
14	import os
15	import re
16	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
17	from datetime import datetime
18	from pathlib import Path
19	from typing import Any, Optional
20	
21	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
22	
23	# ── Study Design Hierarchy ──────────────────────────────────────────
24	
25	STUDY_DESIGN_SCORES: dict[str, float] = {
26	    "MetaAnalysis": 1.00,
27	    "SystematicReview": 0.95,
28	    "meta-analysis": 1.00,
29	    "systematic review": 0.95,
30	    "Review": 0.75,
31	    "ClinicalTrial": 0.85,
32	    "randomized controlled trial": 0.85,
33	    "rct": 0.85,
34	    "CaseReport": 0.40,
35	    "Editorial": 0.20,
36	    "LettersAndComments": 0.15,
37	    "Study": 0.60,
38	    "JournalArticle": 0.65,
39	    "Conference": 0.55,
40	    "Dataset": 0.30,
41	    "Book": 0.50,
42	    "BookSection": 0.45,
43	}
44	
45	# Keywords for inferring study design from title/abstract
46	DESIGN_KEYWORDS: dict[str, float] = {
47	    "meta-analysis": 1.00,
48	    "systematic review": 0.95,
49	    "randomized controlled trial": 0.85,
50	    "randomised controlled trial": 0.85,
51	    "double-blind": 0.85,
52	    "placebo-controlled": 0.85,
53	    "cohort study": 0.70,
54	    "prospective cohort": 0.75,
55	    "retrospective": 0.60,
56	    "cross-sectional": 0.55,
57	    "case-control": 0.60,
58	    "case report": 0.40,
59	    "case series": 0.45,
60	    "narrative review": 0.50,
61	    "scoping review": 0.60,
62	    "umbrella review": 0.95,
63	    "editorial": 0.20,
64	    "commentary": 0.25,
65	    "letter to the editor": 0.15,
66	    "opinion": 0.20,
67	    "pilot study": 0.55,
68	    "observational": 0.55,
69	    "n-of-1": 0.35,
70	}
71	
72	
73	@dataclass
74	class EnhancedSourceScore:
75	    """Full source quality score with all signals."""
76	
77	    # Core signals (from existing source_quality.py)
78	    base_quality_score: float = 0.0
79	
80	    # New signals
81	    study_design: str = "Unknown"
82	    study_design_score: float = 0.5
83	    author_max_hindex: int = 0
84	    author_authority_score: float = 0.0
85	    is_retracted: bool = False
86	    content_completeness: str = "unknown"  # full, abstract_only, paywall_shell, stub
87	    content_completeness_score: float = 0.5
88	
89	    # Metadata
90	    publication_types: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
91	    source_url: str = ""
92	    doi: str = ""
93	
94	    # Final composite
95	    composite_score: float = 0.0
96	
97	    def compute_composite(self) -> float:
98	        """Compute weighted composite score from all signals."""
99	        if self.is_retracted:
100	            self.composite_score = 0.0
101	            return 0.0
102	
103	        # Weights match the plan
104	        score = (
105	            self.base_quality_score * 0.35       # Existing scorer (citations, venue, recency, domain)
106	            + self.study_design_score * 0.20     # Study design hierarchy
107	            + self.author_authority_score * 0.10  # Author h-index
108	            + self.content_completeness_score * 0.10  # Full vs abstract vs stub
109	            + 0.25 * self.base_quality_score     # Double-weight base for stability
110	        )
111	        self.composite_score = min(1.0, max(0.0, score))
112	        return self.composite_score
113	
114	
115	def score_study_design(
116	    publication_types: list[str] | None = None,
117	    title: str = "",
118	    abstract: str = "",
119	) -> tuple[str, float]:
120	    """
121	    Score study design from S2 publicationTypes + keyword detection.
122	
123	    Returns (design_name, score).
124	    """
125	    best_design = "Unknown"
126	    best_score = 0.5
127	
128	    # First: check S2 publicationTypes
129	    if publication_types:
130	        for ptype in publication_types:
131	            if ptype in STUDY_DESIGN_SCORES:
132	                s = STUDY_DESIGN_SCORES[ptype]
133	                if s > best_score:
134	                    best_score = s
135	                    best_design = ptype
136	
137	    # Second: keyword detection in title + abstract
138	    text = f"{title} {abstract}".lower()
139	    for keyword, score in DESIGN_KEYWORDS.items():
140	        if keyword in text and score > best_score:
141	            best_score = score
142	            best_design = keyword.title()
143	
144	    return best_design, best_score
145	
146	
147	def score_author_authority(authors: list[dict] | None = None) -> tuple[int, float]:
148	    """
149	    Score author authority from max h-index across authors.
150	
151	    S2 returns authors with hIndex field when requested.
152	    OpenAlex returns authors with summary_stats.h_index.
153	
154	    Returns (max_hindex, normalized_score).
155	    """
156	    if not authors:
157	        return 0, 0.0
158	
159	    max_h = 0
160	    for author in authors:
161	        # S2 format
162	        h = author.get("hIndex", 0) or 0
163	        # OpenAlex format
164	        if not h:
165	            stats = author.get("summary_stats", {})
166	            h = stats.get("h_index", 0) or 0
167	        if isinstance(h, (int, float)) and h > max_h:
168	            max_h = int(h)
169	
170	    # Normalize: h-index of 40+ is world-class
171	    score = min(1.0, max_h / 40) if max_h > 0 else 0.0
172	    return max_h, score
173	
174	
175	def classify_content_completeness(
176	    content: str,
177	    word_count: int | None = None,
178	) -> tuple[str, float]:
179	    """
180	    Classify fetched content as full, abstract_only, paywall_shell, or stub.
181	
182	    Returns (classification, score).
183	    """
184	    if not content:
185	        return "stub", 0.1
186	
187	    if word_count is None:
188	        word_count = len(content.split())
189	
190	    text_lower = content.lower()
191	
192	    # Check for paywall indicators
193	    paywall_phrases = [
194	        "subscribe to access", "sign in to view", "purchase this article",
195	        "institutional access", "get full text", "buy this article",
196	        "rent this article", "log in to access", "access through your institution",
197	    ]
198	    has_paywall = any(phrase in text_lower for phrase in paywall_phrases)
199	
200	    # Check for section headers (indicates full article)
201	    section_headers = ["introduction", "methods", "results", "discussion",
202	                       "conclusion", "abstract", "background", "materials and methods"]
203	    header_count = sum(1 for h in section_headers if h in text_lower)
204	
205	    # Check for reference list
206	    has_references = bool(re.search(
207	        r"(?:references|bibliography|works cited)\s*\n",
208	        text_lower,
209	    ))
210	
211	    if word_count < 50:
212	        return "stub", 0.1
213	    elif has_paywall and word_count < 500:
214	        return "paywall_shell", 0.2
215	    elif word_count < 300 and header_count < 2:
216	        return "abstract_only", 0.4
217	    elif word_count >= 2000 and header_count >= 3:
218	        return "full", 1.0
219	    elif word_count >= 1000 and (header_count >= 2 or has_references):
220	        return "full", 0.9
221	    elif word_count >= 500:
222	        return "abstract_only", 0.5
223	    else:
224	        return "abstract_only", 0.4
225	
226	
227	def check_retraction(
228	    doi: str = "",
229	    openalex_data: dict | None = None,
230	) -> bool:
231	    """
232	    Check if a paper has been retracted.
233	
234	    Sources (in priority order):
235	    1. OpenAlex is_retracted field (free, already in API response)
236	    2. Retraction Watch CSV (if loaded)
237	    """
238	    # OpenAlex check
239	    if openalex_data:
240	        if openalex_data.get("is_retracted", False):
241	            logger.warning("[source-quality] RETRACTED paper detected via OpenAlex: %s", doi)
242	            return True
243	
244	    # Retraction Watch CSV check
245	    rw_db = _get_retraction_watch_db()
246	    if rw_db and doi:
247	        normalized_doi = doi.lower().strip()
248	        if normalized_doi in rw_db:
249	            logger.warning("[source-quality] RETRACTED paper detected via Retraction Watch: %s", doi)
250	            return True
251	
252	    return False
253	
254	
255	# ── Retraction Watch Database ────────────────────────────────────────
256	
257	_RETRACTION_WATCH_DB: set[str] | None = None
258	_RETRACTION_WATCH_PATH = Path(os.getenv(
259	    "PG_RETRACTION_WATCH_PATH",
260	    "data/retraction_watch_dois.txt",
261	))
262	
263	
264	def _get_retraction_watch_db() -> set[str] | None:
265	    """Load Retraction Watch DOI database (lazy, one-time)."""
266	    global _RETRACTION_WATCH_DB
267	
268	    if _RETRACTION_WATCH_DB is not None:
269	        return _RETRACTION_WATCH_DB
270	
271	    if _RETRACTION_WATCH_PATH.exists():
272	        try:
273	            dois = set()
274	            with open(_RETRACTION_WATCH_PATH, encoding="utf-8") as f:
275	                for line in f:
276	                    line = line.strip().lower()
277	                    if line and not line.startswith("#"):
278	                        dois.add(line)
279	            _RETRACTION_WATCH_DB = dois
280	            logger.info("[source-quality] Loaded Retraction Watch DB: %d DOIs", len(dois))
281	            return dois
282	        except Exception as exc:
283	            logger.warning("[source-quality] Failed to load Retraction Watch DB: %s", exc)
284	
285	    _RETRACTION_WATCH_DB = set()
286	    return _RETRACTION_WATCH_DB
287	
288	
289	# ── Source Quality Gate ──────────────────────────────────────────────
290	
291	QUALITY_GATE_THRESHOLD = float(os.getenv("PG_SOURCE_QUALITY_GATE", "0.3"))
292	
293	
294	def source_quality_gate(score: EnhancedSourceScore) -> bool:
295	    """
296	    Determine if a source passes the quality gate.
297	
298	    Gate: composite >= threshold AND not retracted AND not stub.
299	    """
300	    if score.is_retracted:
301	        return False
302	    if score.content_completeness == "stub":
303	        return False
304	    return score.composite_score >= QUALITY_GATE_THRESHOLD
305	
306	
307	# ── Batch Enrichment ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
308	
309	
310	def enrich_evidence_with_quality(
311	    evidence: list[dict],
312	    fetched_content: list[dict] | None = None,
313	) -> list[dict]:
314	    """
315	    Enrich evidence pieces with source quality scores.
316	
317	    Adds 'source_quality_score' and 'study_design' fields to each evidence piece.
318	    Uses metadata already present on the evidence (from S2/OpenAlex API responses).
319	    """
320	    # Build content lookup for completeness check
321	    content_by_url: dict[str, str] = {}
322	    if fetched_content:
323	        for fc in fetched_content:
324	            url = fc.get("url", "")
325	            content = fc.get("content", "")
326	            if url and content:
327	                content_by_url[url] = content
328	
329	    enriched_count = 0
330	    retracted_count = 0
331	
332	    for ev in evidence:
333	        url = ev.get("source_url", "")
334	        doi = ev.get("doi", "")
335	
336	        # Study design
337	        pub_types = ev.get("publication_types", [])
338	        title = ev.get("source_title", "")
339	        statement = ev.get("statement", "")
340	        design_name, design_score = score_study_design(pub_types, title, statement)
341	
342	        # Author authority
343	        authors = ev.get("authors_detail", ev.get("authors", []))
344	        # authors might be list of strings, not dicts
345	        if authors and isinstance(authors[0], str):
346	            max_h, auth_score = 0, 0.0
347	        else:
348	            max_h, auth_score = score_author_authority(authors)
349	
350	        # Content completeness
351	        content = content_by_url.get(url, "")
352	        completeness, comp_score = classify_content_completeness(content)
353	
354	        # Retraction check
355	        retracted = check_retraction(doi)
356	        if retracted:
357	            retracted_count += 1
358	
359	        # Build enhanced score
360	        base_score = ev.get("source_confidence", ev.get("relevance_score", 0.5))
361	        enhanced = EnhancedSourceScore(
362	            base_quality_score=base_score,
363	            study_design=design_name,
364	            study_design_score=design_score,
365	            author_max_hindex=max_h,
366	            author_authority_score=auth_score,
367	            is_retracted=retracted,
368	            content_completeness=completeness,
369	            content_completeness_score=comp_score,
370	            publication_types=pub_types,
371	            source_url=url,
372	            doi=doi,
373	        )
374	        enhanced.compute_composite()
375	
376	        # Attach to evidence
377	        ev["source_quality_enhanced"] = enhanced.composite_score
378	        ev["study_design"] = design_name
379	        ev["study_design_score"] = design_score
380	        ev["content_completeness"] = completeness
381	        ev["is_retracted"] = retracted
382	        ev["author_max_hindex"] = max_h
383	
384	        enriched_count += 1
385	
386	    logger.info(
387	        "[source-quality] Enriched %d evidence pieces (%d retracted)",
388	        enriched_count, retracted_count,
389	    )
390	
391	    return evidence
392
